text.title: Event-Driven Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Event-Driven Architecture` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every consumer MUST be idempotent — keyed by event_id, dedup window declared. Non-idempotent consumer = REJECT.
rule.rationale: All major brokers deliver at-least-once; idempotency is non-optional.

rule.statement: Event names are past-tense business outcomes (OrderPlaced, PaymentCaptured). Imperative (PlaceOrder) or vague (OrderUpdated) = REJECT.
rule.rationale: Past-tense names lock semantics; imperative leaks command intent.

rule.statement: Every saga step ships its compensating action in the same PR as the happy path.
rule.rationale: Untested compensations fail at incident time.

rule.statement: Every event has a registered schema (Avro / Protobuf / JSON Schema) — producers register; consumers validate.
rule.rationale: Schemaless events cause silent consumer breakage on producer change.

rule.statement: Every consumer route MUST have a DLQ + poison-message detector. Skipped record visible, not lost.
rule.rationale: Silent record drop = silent data loss.

rule.statement: Production topic retention configured for replay (per-domain SLA). Sub-day retention = REJECT for business-event topics.
rule.rationale: Short retention defeats the main argument for events.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
